textByHash.be1977afac2c565ac9f644ab43eae69de1f8dad6: That's right, who wants to know...?
textByHash.a059b7ada4b13e065ac377af7fc76f2bd6c5f437: Ah yes, I recognise you! You're the fellow who found that strange artefact about Zaros for the museum, aren't you? What can I do for you now?
textByHash.b97c844ca658b0fc9523ac32f5bdf7ceab7e4ea8: Then... you better go get them for me?
textByHash.3e0646b0b0ea10f566bc0ae4d4401535364a4686: You spoke to the legendary Asgarnia Smith??? Quickly, let me see what he had to give you! He is always at the forefront of archaeological breakthroughs!
textByHash.5ad679a22aa5359e3f8a79c8695e78c0e57cc54b: This... this is fascinating! These cuneiforms seem to predate even the settlement we are excavating here... Yes, yes, this is most interesting indeed!
textByHash.0151531602efdb73a47ac476bebb3180ea3108c0: Well, I am not familiar with this particular language, but the similarities inherent in the pictographs seem to show a prevalent trend towards a syllabary consistent with the phonemes we have discovered in this excavation!
textByHash.e9e41bf95f96f88080b2e39ff4266a4dbb18d478: Well, unfortunately this is the only example of this particular language I have ever seen, but I might be able to make a rough translation, of sorts...
textByHash.5dd9d4b610e1e16e84b80778f5fec3bbfeaf436c: It might be slightly obscure on the finer details, but it should be good enough to understand the rough meaning of what was originally written. Please, just wait a moment, I will write up what I can
textByHash.4569c004508ca27ac167c6524d6f7507dfd76c89: translate into a journal for you. Then you can take it back to Asgarnia, I think he will be extremely interested in the translation!
textByHash.a026273a28e212c6bafb9f11f8a86a3311858700: There you go, that book contains the sum of my translating ability. If you would be so kind as to take that back to Asgarnia, I think it will reassure him that he is on the
textByHash.461d3c4f09de171fa1189ffe404dfcaffcefcda5: right track for a find of great archaeological importance
textByHash.b5905f1e57a5cfd37a8bfef6c3fce57f78d1118b: What can I say? It's a skill I picked up through my many years of taking field notes!
textByHash.8869d719bce799f4583bf2476d75cf5a1152e8f5: Hello again. Was that translation any use to Asgarnia?
textByHash.f6ee25387a7ab3256bc1d45d8ef34daa7641e50b: Whyever not? You're the strangest delivery boy I've ever met!
textByHash.0d3fcfeb6c0851162dbc6159b7630271d37820e3: Oh, no matter, I mostly remember what it said anyway! Here you go!
textByHash.29eff2202a0f9bc966ccd079bab8f294aa9fee81: Hello again. How goes the archaeology?
textByHash.c81aa2ee5f1210c5e8682f905ed4a16374dc0cc9: Zarosian? Like that artefact you found for us?
textByHash.6fd8792fd8044f000cc4643fa61f501b4e90e136: Sounds like an exciting discovery, but I'm afraid I can't help. This 'Zaros' is still mostly a mystery to me. In fact, I'd say you probably know more than I do!
textByHash.647b02b560ba78dddaa23211da9141f82a44c860: Well you may well be in luck. We had a new arrival at the Digsite recently who claims to be a Zarosian expert!
textByHash.3e12524aac5ac0ecfd57b8cba9e5b6d5fa37c629: Quite impressive for one of those to exist considering the greatest historians and archaeologists in the world has no knowledge on Zaros until very recently...
textByHash.7bf24a59c6b43d4fa8b6a8ed7349e17b8067d1f9: He's set up a new dig on the eastern edge of the Digsite. You're looking for... Ah, what was his name again...
textByHash.da43b1bc5c9f1e6a6e2fba6184af5a3e057810fe: Banikan! You'll be wanting to speak to Dr Banikan!
textByHash.4335d0619685ce6a04bb633018c471b5d874d403: Hello again. Did you speak to Dr Banikan?
textByHash.6ba788d0d9d51d041e6208faf9d2bb07facfada9: He's set up a new dig on the eastern edge of the Digsite. You'll likely find him there.
textByHash.b4e4484a463e3300eee8e8b1c072f6ef31eb71c9: Unusual... This object doesn't appear right...
textByHash.12ff2d1fd544e63367ea55602d8f36c6f1289e1f: I wonder... Let me check my guide... Could it be? Surely not!
textByHash.814e8535f19a1e05f782e277cde265493703be02: From the markings on it, it seems to be a ceremonial ornament to a god named...
textByHash.2df3df3a9b7f480e0da6380c8f414e2f55e2514e: ...Zaros? I haven't heard much about him before. This is a great discovery; we know very little of the pagan gods that people worshipped.
textByHash.87148bc062169b90c8f4f8bf4ba6da3ea81e0d66: There is some strange writing embossed upon it - it says, 'Zaros will return and wreak his vengeance upon Zamorak the pretender.'
textByHash.03a5c4c693dae5a95aa9f04b27e06051d2431816: I wonder what it means by that? Some silly superstition, probably.
textByHash.77413755c53956b854f9a02a9f2db87053ee9f16: Still, I wonder what this is doing around here. I'll tell you what; as you have found this, I will allow you to use the private dig shafts.
textByHash.f6e0c12355d5693713fa4a3477d92483b964173b: You obviously have a keen eye. Take this letter and give it to one of the workmen, and they will allow you to use them.
textByHash.71480cc7af50c5d4e145320cae42a37384f6d0d9: Good day to you. My name is Terry Balando; I am an expert on digsite finds.
textByHash.8cc057c7a8e1be193519d57f946127c7313afda1: I am employed by the museum in Varrock to oversee all finds at this digsite. Anything you find must be reported to me.
textByHash.907bee10a5750e11159102773c5c00db4680a9ff: Can I help you at all?
textByHash.97a74ec4c4184f177cd7f553ad2dbfc500790ab5: No you haven't! You have one in your pack!
textByHash.f555cfe177f5b221387ec23455234d9b8bf7cd4d: No you haven't! You have one in your bank!
textByHash.a0a9fff51d51bba613d29e1fd582d7e60b4169e3: I can't believe it! I go to all the effort of writing you a letter of recommendation and you lose it! Here, I'll write another... Don't lose it again!
textByHash.ceaa1f8865fed590ce32cb37479cf22bd252e836: There's no point in giving me this back!
textByHash.7df1e78ddce03018c215878a5440489f63138236: Oh, hello. I hope you're getting on well in the dig shafts.
textByHash.d38edffa98af2894157f3304795835796aaac2e9: This is a VERY dangerous liquid called nitroglycerin. Be careful how you handle it. Don't drop it or it will explode!
textByHash.6f9a2851a9f36c6cd99b3028356c429c2045929b: Really, you do find the most unusual items. I know what this is, it's a strong chemical called ammonium nitrate. Why you want this I'll never know...
textByHash.2038f8b9d3ea36d3df75e27c50d26936f8dd6a10: This is an amazing discovery! All this while we were convinced that no other race had lived here.
textByHash.56bba7917b0a459d571f110d14be36acffdb041a: It seems the followers of Saradomin have tried to cover up the evidence of the Zaros altar. This whole city must have been built over it!
textByHash.bd09852fd86f6c3ccc34d33b0d29bb3be42fcdbe: Thanks for your help; your sharp eyes have spotted what many have missed. Here, take this gold as your reward.
textByHash.bf2e6d373f9ab33d3390d6a0c048353c8830aad8: Okay, give it to me and I'll have a look for you.
textByHash.962e1ad770c6ab9fb2512655bccb0e15f7b34611: Good, let me know if you find anything unusual.
textByHash.1691e631e846da336cdf293e43f4b64864e447e4: Yes, indeed; I am studying the lives of the settlers. During the end of the third age there used to be a great city at the site.
textByHash.fc25df241905fdfb158256545bb3e3a3daa01163: Its inhabitants were humans, supporters of the god Saradomin. It's not recorded what happened to the community here.
textByHash.ee63e81ae17d4363c9dfa0a78463d7edcfcf94b7: I suspect nobody has lived here for over a millennium!
textByHash.ca3081b36c91d208ddc21cbcbcc50200826addad: Of course! Let's see now... Rock picks are for splitting rocks or scraping away soil; you can get one from a cupboard in the Education Centre.
textByHash.bbfe67e76812f7a5e75a0d78bd8aa01878e2c1c3: I think you'll find them scattered about pretty much everywhere, but I know you can get one from a cupboard somewhere in the Education Centre, just like the rock pick!
textByHash.a22e26ccc1f708f633198b986c13746ad73a9d7a: We have a bit of a shortage of those at the moment. You could try borrowing one from a workman on the site... but I don't think they'd give it willingly.
textByHash.1f8a016b2b4fe74e58cac476e9eda765a602e7dd: Ahh... that you must earn by passing your exams! The examiner holds those.
textByHash.9a6e48fa7221ef2173dd655fe48d90e5462c98cb: Hello again. I am now studying this mysterious altar and its inhabitants. The markings are strange...
textByHash.9dff1c25020b03e954ba2e282af160fd8aeaff67: It refers to a god I have never heard of before, named Zaros. It must be some pagan superstition.
textByHash.d1946fd4b5ccd57ed1cd2243917e34cce876720d: That was a great find; who knows what other secrets lie buried beneath the surface of our land...
textByHash.4bac7fc87d2c5799710886f5c27cca155327ba24: That staff is incredible! It's the same symbol that was on that talisman you found here. Does this mean you've found out more about Zaros?
textByHash.2a40472e8bd07dd0c1ac96cdb16edcc3d1a1d61d: So you're the one who found out about that. I've heard the story from my friends in the museum. Well done on being able to wield such an impressive symbol.
textByHash.d05586dc019ef646c92fa78c7f55bc88a5f08fd7: Nitroglycerin! This is a dangerous substance. This is normally mixed with other chemicals to produce a potent compound.
textByHash.20f38202f9ea4856e61022cd682ed14343373d10: Be sure not to drop it! That stuff is highly volatile...
textByHash.a11194bc2fd27ff3b63b3847492a667ac74906db: Really, you do find the most unusual items. I know what this is - it's a strong chemical called ammonium nitrate. Why you want this I'll never know...
textByHash.24993910530645ed026e163592761839b405ab06: Hmm, that looks dangerous. Handle it carefully and don't drop it!
textByHash.047d1e488e40ecdd1a69858cff0ea698b38ee433: Seriously, I think you have a death wish! What on earth are you going to do with that stuff?
textByHash.1214fa883b1d9cef0d971f46ed6b5877bfcafedb: What have you concocted now? Just be careful when playing with chemicals!
textByHash.fb65377faa44c540d21e2128a65f10bb31bcfb21: I can't do much with these nuggets yet. Come back when you have 3 and I will exchange them for you.
textByHash.4d4248d86e21d8a1db757019c666309ced19b783: Good – that's three; I can exchange them for normal gold now. You can get this refined and make a profit!
textByHash.2aa3a2298d4b5cc9449b6850451e182269ee619f: Hmm, yes; I wondered why this race were so well dressed! It looks like they had mastery of needlework.
textByHash.e3f77649aba6e45ecd10db3ba9645587c03791db: Ew! Throw them away this instant!
textByHash.289f3a7d6d01eca5dc512cb29091e9d3e7eddca2: Hey you should be careful of that. It might cut your fingers, throw it away!
textByHash.1550cf2f006dba576a8bad01180b3397742302ce: No doubt this arrow was shot by a strong warrior – it's split in half! It is not a valuable object though.
textByHash.93a803aa10eb89ac19855db2b7a4d771dabd8fb7: I have no need for panning trays!
textByHash.efb17095c3aa08136e55c04a448c41e5bdd31492: Have you searched this tray yet?
textByHash.9f62933195ae6b9b31924d5571a28555472c7dc9: It may contain something; I don't want to get my hands dirty.
textByHash.6aeaa69b11f83a11fe4bc85afceae11fa0e85e0c: Ah, yes – a fine bone example... no noticeable fractures... and in good condition. These are common cow bones, however; they have no archaeological value.
textByHash.fbbeefd8b04ecdedc9d5ad5e7fba87e6aef716ae: Let's have a look. Ah, I think these are from the nobility, perhaps a royal servant?
textByHash.db23fcd641fbfeedff61de9cb194e7ed0ec18011: What a shame it's cracked; this looks like it would have been a good sample.
textByHash.7533e866542cef12f0abb1854e276f1d1cd144df: Oh, an old tooth. It looks like it has come from a mighty being. Pity there are no tooth fairies around here!
textByHash.59351a989e6d8104044433bcbfed6b9d454c445d: Oh, it's very rusty isn't it? I'm not sure this sword belongs here, it looks very out of place.
textByHash.5345824d4ed29934b2d37fd1e802963f7ae5e86e: Look at this... Interesting... This appears to belong to a cleric of some kind; certainly not a follower of Saradomin, however.
textByHash.8e865ec8e18da01eadbac485721c96e0eee62af3: I wonder if there was another civilization here before the Saradominists?
textByHash.6bf5db4eefeb6422ae5b6848b55c07cff8999178: It looks like the wearer of this fought a mighty battle.
textByHash.0c35b564f65901731dfe07ff67460229f1f6ac56: How unusual. This armour doesn't seem to match with the other finds. Keep looking.
textByHash.eda5e45d8afb9c212ffcb4faa101138dfdee1a76: Yes, many parts are discovered. The inhabitants of these parts were great potters.
textByHash.91a6f5bb6897246c0c1aa2bdd1e4ba942af93385: No, no, silly. They were known for their skill with clay.
textByHash.bbadedf4c6889e92a52af58b5097fb37ea4c2d98: Let me see. This is a belt buckle. Not so unusual - I should imagine it came from a guard.
textByHash.4aa7f2283cb03f2761ca2f7ea5133d825adff926: Hmm, an interesting find; an animal skull for sure. Another student found one just like this today.
textByHash.44af79590dc82d20d4ae0c62b3f2b77d5c8803df: Looks like an award cup for some small find. Perhaps it belongs to one of the students?
textByHash.657011c4c6a6a929e931dd406808c17cb5eb82f4: Why, it looks like a teddy bear to me. Perhaps someone's lucky mascot!
textByHash.9be9eb94af3ed9a78d216350e25e2caf43251e71: Ah yes, an old boot. Not really an ancient artefact, is it?
textByHash.5082676e049b787998688d7e375403b5991e948a: I don't need another tablet! One is enough, thank you.
